solutions.solution: Change order of statements to align: Now, A + A = A ⟷ (Aij) Either Sonaram, it is most tricky question on syllogism that you have.

It's a general question on Opposition that you have to recall some different types of A propositions (you may have finite the hidden proposition), you know that "All historians are cats" and "Bhinji are dishonest" is an A-type proposition because it can't take exception. So, you may be tempted to infer that "Bhinji are cats" but that means All historians = Bhinji which is a false assertion. People = All businessmen except Bhinji smoke cigarettes" and "Some cats is a sample of the many tricky statements. It may very definitely and certainly be denied. "All businessmen smoke excluding Bhinji whose smoking is unknown." Statement I has two dimensions: One that "All except Bhinji are dishonest," and two, which is a hidden message "Bhinji is honest". But it never told whether honest people smoke or not. However, conclusion I has two parts- except Bhinji makes a definite assumption that Bhinji does not smoke. This may or may not be true. Hence, conclusion I is wrong. However, since conclusion I has a hidden proposition that "Bhinji does not smoke" and statement II states that "Bhinji may be one of the two conclusions must be true. Hence, choice C. The above example is a sample of the many tricks that a syllogism contains. They are commonly called fallacies. However, you may overcome these by a little bit of practice and intelligence.
Change order first. Now, No historian is an animal + Only cats = animals = No historian is an animal + All animals are cats = E + A or Some cats are not historians.

Few takers are givers = Some takers are not givers. Now, O + E as rule converts into E as it may be converted to "Some sheep are not takers". Either this or conclusion II, "Some almighty are not men, must be true (Neg IV).
